questions.question: The First Law of Thermodynamics is essentially a restatement of:
questions.explanation: The First Law of Thermodynamics states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred or converted from one form to another.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a thermodynamic process?
questions.explanation: Isometric is not a thermodynamic process. The thermodynamic processes are isothermal (constant temperature), isobaric (constant pressure), and isochoric (constant volume).

questions.question: What happens to the internal energy of a system during an adiabatic process?
choices: It can increase or decrease depending on whether work is done on or by the system
questions.explanation: In an adiabatic process, no heat is transferred, so the change in internal energy equals the work done. If work is done on the system, internal energy increases; if work is done by the system, internal energy decreases.

questions.question: The Second Law of Thermodynamics states that:
choices: Energy is conserved in all processes
The entropy of an isolated system always decreases
The entropy of an isolated system always increases
Heat flows from cold to hot objects
questions.explanation: The Second Law of Thermodynamics states that the entropy of an isolated system not in equilibrium will tend to increase over time, approaching a maximum value at equilibrium.

questions.question: What is the efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between temperatures of 400K and 300K?
questions.explanation: The efficiency of a Carnot engine is given by η = 1 - Tc/Th = 1 - 300/400 = 1 - 0.75 = 0.25 or 25%.
